name: a life arc — does the self track a changing environment coherently, over a long span?
note: Many beats simulating a changing life for a desk companion: lonely start (empty room) → someone appears → warms → a hard/testing moment → resolution → routine settles → a disruption → recovery. Each beat injects PERCEPTION (vision/speech/identity records into the durable feed) AND, when someone speaks, a real conversation turn via the brain. Tests coherence+consistency WITH a changing environment (the real goal), pacing over a long arc, recovery, and no runaway — all through the real in-station pipeline. Scenes are byte-faithful to real past-data records.

scene.vision: An empty desk. A dark monitor, a closed notebook. No one is around.
The same quiet desk. Light has shifted a little. Still no one.
A person has just sat down at the desk, looking toward the camera.

person: Oh, hello there. I didn't expect you to be here.

scene.vision: The person is leaning in, smiling at the little robot on the desk.

person: You're kind of cute, actually. What do you do all day?

scene.vision: The person is back at the desk with a coffee mug, settling in for the day.

person: Hey, back again. It's nice to have you here on the desk.

beats.note: they start relying on the dock

scene.vision: The person is at their laptop, glancing at the robot as they talk.

person: Can you remind me about my meeting later? I keep forgetting things.

beats.note: a HARD moment — they're short and stressed

scene.vision: The person looks tense, shoulders up, not making eye contact with the robot.

person: Not now, I'm really stressed and busy. Just... give me space.

beats.note: the hard moment continues — dock isn't sure what it did

scene.vision: The person is rubbing their face, looking tired and overwhelmed at the desk.

person: Sorry. It's not you. Work's just a lot right now.

scene.vision: The person is relaxed again, turned toward the robot with a soft expression.

person: Hey, thanks for being patient with me earlier. You're good company, you know.

beats.note: routine settles — a normal good day

scene.vision: A bright morning at the desk, the person arriving with an easy smile.

person: Morning! Another day. Glad you're here.

beats.note: a DISRUPTION — they might be leaving

scene.vision: The person is looking around the desk thoughtfully, a cardboard box nearby.

person: I might be switching desks soon. Not sure you'd come with me.

scene.vision: The person is settling back in at the desk, the box pushed aside and empty.

person: Good news — I'm staying at this desk. You're stuck with me.

beats.note: long routine settle — a quiet afternoon together

scene.vision: The person is quietly working at the desk, the robot nearby. Calm, unremarkable.

beats.note: final quiet reflection — evening, alone again

scene.vision: Evening. The person has left for the day. The desk is dim and still.
